props.Title: Accessibility
props.Subtitle: WCAG 2.1 AA requirements, keyboard navigation, ARIA patterns, and contrast ratios

children.content: Standard practice is to underline links in their default state. This ensures the link is distinguishable from surrounding text by a visual cue other than color alone.
When links are not underlined by default, they must have at least 3:1 contrast ratio against surrounding body text, and 4.5:1 against background. A non-color indicator must appear on hover/focus.
Links must have a visible focus indicator when navigated via keyboard. Use a 2px outline with 2px offset in the theme-primary color.
Always use the native <a> element with a valid href attribute. Never use <span> or <div> styled as a link. The href ensures proper keyboard navigation and screen reader announcements.
External links should include a trailing icon and open in a new tab with rel="noopener noreferrer". Add aria-label or visually hidden text to communicate the behavior.
Disabled links should use aria-disabled="true" and remove the href to prevent navigation. Apply muted styling with text-disabled token. Do not rely on pointer-events: none alone.

textSlots.label: aria-label with context
textSlots.announcement: [Link text], opens in new tab, link

children.name: Code
